reasoning: The 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship is a 64-player, single-elimination tournament. For any average player, the baseline probability of winning is 1/64 (~1.56%). Because Stephen Chidwick is one of the top poker professionals in the world, his skill advantage increases his chances. However, heads-up No-Limit Hold'em has very high variance, and a player must win six consecutive matches to claim the title. Even assuming an optimistic 60% win probability per match against a mixed field of pros and amateurs, his chance of winning six consecutive matches would be roughly (0.60)^6 ≈ 4.6%. Without any concrete leak or spoiler confirming his victory, his probability remains around the base rate for an elite pro. I am predicting 5%, the lowest allowed prediction given the strict 0.05-0.95 bounds.
